# -*- sh -*-
# See http://rigaux.org/language-study/diagram.html for the format of this file
#claude: auto-generated by Claude Code (inspired from my other lineage.txt)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GUI toolkit / widget library lineage
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

#src: wikipedia articles on different programs
#src: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_widget_toolkits
#src: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Widget_toolkit

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-Historical
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Alto Smalltalk GUI
  1973
  # MVC pattern originated here (Smalltalk-76/80)
  # Xerox PARC, first real widget toolkit concept

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Historical (UNIX / X Window)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

# X Toolkit Intrinsics (Xt) is the foundation layer, not a widget set itself

*Xt (X Toolkit Intrinsics)
  1987
  SY:2
  # part of X11, provides base widget class and geometry management
  # Xt is the framework; Athena and Motif are widget sets on top

*Athena (Xaw)
  1987
  Xt (X Toolkit Intrinsics)
  SY:2
  # MIT, very simple/ugly widgets, shipped with X11
  # but foundational, first real X widget set
*Xaw3d
  1992
  Athena (Xaw)
  # 3D look for Athena widgets
neXtaw
  Athena (Xaw)
  # NeXT look for Athena widgets

*Motif
  1989
  Xt (X Toolkit Intrinsics)
  SY:6
  # by OSF (Open Software Foundation), proprietary until 2012
  # CDE (Common Desktop Environment) built on top
  # "standard" commercial UNIX look
Motif 1.2
  1992
Motif 2.0
  1994
Open Motif
  2000
  # license opened for open source platforms
*Lesstif
  1995
  Motif
  SY:2
  # free reimplementation of Motif, LGPL

InterViews
  1988
  Xt (X Toolkit Intrinsics)
  # Stanford, Mark Linton, one of the first C++ GUI toolkits
  # influential on Motif and later C++ toolkits

XForms
  1993
  Xt (X Toolkit Intrinsics)
  # simpler than Motif, used by LyX
  # draws directly on Xlib, bypasses Xt actually

XView
  1988
  SY:6
  # Sun, OpenLook toolkit for X Window
  # successor to SunView (which was not X-based)
OLIT
  1989
  Xt (X Toolkit Intrinsics)
  SY:6
  # OpenLook Intrinsics Toolkit, Sun/AT&T, Xt-based alternative to XView

*Tk
  1991
  SY:2
  # by Tcl creator (John Ousterhout), for Tcl scripting
  # later got bindings for Perl, Python (Tkinter), Ruby, etc.
Tk 4.0
  1995
Tk 8.0
  1997
  # native look and feel
*Tk 8.5
  2007
  # Ttk (themed Tk)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plan9
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*libpanel
  1995
  Tk
  SY:1
  # Plan 9, simple widget library on top of libdraw
  # used by mothra (web browser)

libframe
  1993
  SY:1
  # Plan 9, text frame widget, used by sam and acme

libcontrol
  2002
  SY:1
  # Plan 9, channel-based widget library (4th edition)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source (cross-platform)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*GTK
  1996
  Motif
  SY:2
  # originally "GIMP Toolkit", started 1996, 1.0 in 1998
  # C-based with GObject type system
  # replaced Motif for GIMP because of Motif licensing
*GTK 2.0
  2002
  # Pango, ATK, new theme engine
*GTK 3.0
  2011
*GTK 4.0
  2020

*Qt
  1995
  Motif
  SY:2
  # Trolltech, C++ with MOC (meta-object compiler) for signals/slots
  # dual-licensed (commercial + GPL/LGPL)
*Qt 2.0
  1999
*Qt 3.0
  2001
*Qt 4.0
  2005
  # major rewrite, Arthur graphics, model/view
*Qt 5.0
  2012
  # QML, scene graph
*Qt 6.0
  2020

wxWidgets
  1992
  Motif
  SY:2
  # originally wxWindows (renamed 2004 due to Microsoft trademark)
  # wraps native widgets (Motif, then GTK, Win32, Cocoa)
  # started as Motif/XView wrapper
wxWidgets 2.0
  1997

FLTK
  1998
  XForms
  SY:2
  # Fast Light Toolkit, by Bill Spitzak (who worked on SGI forms library)
  # very lightweight, draws its own widgets
  # inspired by Forms Library from SGI
FLTK 1.1
  2002
FLTK 1.3
  2011


EFL (Enlightenment Foundation Libraries)
  2002
  SY:2
  # Evas canvas + Edje theming + Elementary widgets
  # used in Tizen (Samsung)

Fox Toolkit
  1997
  SY:2
  # C++, draws its own widgets

GNUstep
  1994
  # free implementation of OpenStep/Cocoa API
  # AppKit widgets

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Industry
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ------------
# Apple
# ------------

MacApp
  1985
  Alto Smalltalk GUI
  SY:6
  # first application framework for Macintosh, Object Pascal
  # Macintosh Toolbox was the lower-level API

MacToolbox
  1984
  SY:6
  # original Macintosh widget API (controls, dialogs, menus)
  # procedural C/Pascal API

GEM
  1985
  SY:6
  # Digital Research (Gary Kildall), used on Atari ST and early PCs

PowerPlant
  1995
  MacApp
  SY:6
  # Metrowerks, C++ framework for Mac

Carbon
  1999
  MacToolbox
  SY:6
  # C API, bridge from classic Mac to Mac OS X

*Cocoa (AppKit)
  1989
  SY:6
  # originally NeXTStep AppKit (1989), became Cocoa in Mac OS X (2001)
  # Objective-C, Interface Builder

UIKit
  2008
  Cocoa (AppKit)
  SY:6
  # iOS, touch-oriented

*SwiftUI
  2019
  UIKit,Cocoa (AppKit)
  SY:6
  # declarative, reactive

# ------------
# Microsoft
# ------------

*Windows SDK (USER/GDI)
  1985
  SY:6
  # Win16 API: CreateWindow, message loop, WndProc
  # the original Windows widget system

*MFC
  1992
  Windows SDK (USER/GDI)
  SY:6
  # Microsoft Foundation Classes, C++ wrapper around Win32 API
  # document/view architecture
MFC 4.0
  1995
  # shipped with Visual C++ 4.0

OWL
  1991
  Windows SDK (USER/GDI)
  SY:6
  # Borland ObjectWindows Library, competed with MFC

VCL
  1995
  OWL
  SY:6
  # Borland Visual Component Library, for Delphi
  # Object Pascal, visual form designer

ATL/WTL
  1996
  Windows SDK (USER/GDI)
  SY:6
  # Active Template Library / Windows Template Library
  # lightweight alternative to MFC

Windows Forms
  2002
  MFC
  SY:6
  # .NET, C#, visual designer, wraps Win32 controls

*WPF
  2006
  Windows Forms
  SY:6
  # Windows Presentation Foundation, XAML, vector-based
  # DirectX rendering, data binding

UWP (Universal Windows Platform)
  2015
  WPF
  SY:6
  # XAML-based, sandboxed apps

WinUI 3
  2021
  UWP (Universal Windows Platform)
  SY:6
  # successor to UWP controls, part of Windows App SDK

# ------------
# Java
# ------------

*AWT
  1996
  SY:6
  # Abstract Window Toolkit, wraps native widgets
  # shipped with JDK 1.0

*Swing
  1998
  AWT
  SY:6
  # "lightweight" (draws own widgets), pluggable look and feel
  # MVC-ish architecture

SWT
  2003
  AWT
  SY:6
  # Eclipse Standard Widget Toolkit, wraps native like AWT but better
  # JNI to GTK/Win32/Cocoa

JavaFX
  2008
  Swing
  SY:6
  # scene graph, CSS styling, FXML
JavaFX 2.0
  2011

# ------------
# Other industry
# ------------

Turbo Vision
  1990
  SY:6
  # Borland, TUI (text-mode) widget library for Turbo Pascal/C++

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Web / Electron era
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

XUL
  1998
  # Mozilla, XML-based UI language, powered Firefox UI for ~20 years

*Electron
  2013
  # Chromium + Node.js, HTML/CSS widgets
  # originally Atom Shell (by GitHub)

*React Native
  2015
  # wraps native widgets via JavaScript bridge

*Flutter
  2018
  # Google, Dart, draws own widgets (Skia-based)
  # cross-platform (mobile, desktop, web)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Recent / Modern
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Dear ImGui
  2014
  # immediate mode GUI, C++, for game tools/debug
  # very popular in gamedev

*Nuklear
  2015
  Dear ImGui
  # single-header C immediate mode GUI

egui
  2020
  Dear ImGui
  # Rust immediate mode GUI

Iced
  2019
  # Rust, Elm-inspired, retained mode

Slint
  2021
  Qt
  # Rust/C++, declarative markup (.slint files)
  # ex-Qt developers

Tauri
  2022
  Electron
  # Rust + native webview, lightweight alternative to Electron

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Language-specific
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Python
PyQt
  1998
  Qt
PySide
  2009
  Qt

# Rust
Druid
  2019
  # Rust, data-driven

# Go
Fyne
  2018
  # Go, material design

# OCaml
LablGTK
  1999
  GTK
  # OCaml bindings to GTK

LablTk
  1996
  Tk
  # OCaml bindings to Tk

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exotic / TUI
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*curses
  1977
  # terminal UI library (not really a widget toolkit)
*ncurses
  1993
  curses
  SY:2

S-Lang
  1992
  curses
  # text UI library

CDK
  1999
  ncurses
  # Curses Development Kit, widget library on top of ncurses

Newt
  1996
  S-Lang
  # Red Hat, used in anaconda installer

*dialog
  1994
  ncurses
  SY:2
  # TUI dialog boxes, ubiquitous in Linux installers and sysadmin tools

*notcurses
  2019
  ncurses
  # modern TUI library

Textual
  2021
  # Python, by Will McGuigan (author of Rich)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Wrappers / Bindings layers
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

GObject Introspection
  2009
  GTK
  # language-agnostic bindings for GTK/GNOME

Vala
  2006
  GTK
  # GNOME-oriented language that compiles to C/GObject
